description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped outdoor air conditioning unit with a slightly sloped top and a weathered, off-white exterior. The front face features a large, circular fan grille with a radial mesh pattern, likely made of metal, which serves as the primary air intake or exhaust. The sides and back are plain, with visible smudges and dirt marks, indicating exposure to outdoor elements. The top surface is flat and extends slightly beyond the main body, forming a shallow overhang. The overall construction appears robust, with the main housing likely made of painted metal or coated steel. There are no visible control panels, handles, or brand logos on any side.
description: A weathered, rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit with a large circular fan grille on the front and a plain, off-white metal housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent circular fan grille with a radial mesh pattern and a weathered, off-white surface.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, slightly stained metal panel with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a flat, unadorned metal surface with some smudges and weathering.
description_view_3: Right side view, similar to the left, with a plain metal panel and the edge of the fan grille just visible.
attribute_grille_pattern: Circular radial mesh pattern with evenly spaced, radiating metal slats forming a fan-like structure.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large circular vent on the front, covered by a radial mesh grille; no other visible vents.
